scenes.text: Most topic to video tools ask you to trust a black box.
scenes.visualDirection: A terminal window with a vague generate command dissolves into a messy folder of unknown files.

scenes.text: Content Machine makes every stage inspectable: script, audio, timestamps, visuals, captions, render metadata, and review.
scenes.visualDirection: Show a clean artifact tree where each pipeline stage writes one clear JSON or media file.

scenes.text: Agents read skills to understand the job, flows to coordinate steps, and harness scripts to run the repo through JSON standard input.
scenes.visualDirection: Three labeled cards: skills, flows, harness scripts, connected by arrows into a short-form video timeline.

scenes.text: That means a failed caption check or weak visual plan is visible before anyone pretends the short is ready.
scenes.visualDirection: A publish-prep report highlights pass and fail checks beside a video preview.

scenes.text: Clone it, run doctor report, then generate a short you can actually debug.
scenes.visualDirection: End card with clone, doctor-report, and generate-short commands stacked as a simple checklist.

reasoning: Offline-friendly self-demo script for proving the Content Machine artifact chain without API calls. The smoke path uses mock audio, mock visuals, and mock render output; the full self-demo request should be used for a publishable demo candidate.
title: Content Machine in 30 Seconds
hook: Topic-to-video should not be a black box.
cta: Clone it, run diagnostics, then generate a short.
